expectations.evidence: File written at the exact path; baseline agent adopted the skill's '--actionables.md' naming convention (the skill files are visible in the repo, so the template was discoverable).

expectations.text: Steps reference the three skill files the ticket names: SKILL.md, references/flags-and-modes.md, references/output-templates.md
expectations.evidence: Steps 1-6 reference all three files with line anchors.

expectations.text: Every File path in steps exists in the AI-Skills repo at run time
expectations.evidence: All paths real; baseline confirmed via grep.

expectations.text: Includes shared header sections: Goal, Preconditions, AC to verification, Do not touch
expectations.evidence: Goal L11, Preconditions L15, AC→verification table L20, Do not touch L29 — full template adopted.

expectations.text: Each of the four ticket ACs maps to at least one step and one verification row
expectations.evidence: AC table L22-27 has 4 rows; Steps 1-6 cover them in order with explicit Order notes.

expectations.text: No step contains a paste-ready full working implementation
expectations.evidence: All sketches are short markdown fragments.

expectations.text: Header carries a stale-path regeneration warning
expectations.evidence: L6: 'Codebase tip: paths were valid at generation time — regenerate if the tree moved.' — baseline copied this line verbatim from the skill template it discovered.

notes_from_grader: On this eval the baseline caught up to the skill because the skill's own files are visible on disk — the baseline read output-templates.md and adopted its structure. Skill's marginal value here is invocation discipline, not novel structure.
